extra.reason: Shipped: per-session Claude config isolation via a claude wrapper + CLAUDE_CONFIG_DIR seeding + init-time snapshot + zshrc git-author. Fixed via option (b): issues.jsonl is a lagging derived export by design (auto-export structurally can't run in a memory-bearing repo — shrink guard). Shipped: net-new `rc agent <cage> --name=<handle> -- <command>` mechanical lever (opaque verbatim tmux-new-session pass-through via "${cmd_args[@]}" array forwarding; duplicate-name surfaces tmux error non-zero without clobber; zero policy/intelligence per ADR-006 D7/ADR-002), reusing existing rc sessions/--kill/attach. NEEDS_CONTAINER e2e tests/test-multi-agent-levers.sh (wired into run-host.sh) green against a real cage — exit 0, all 5 assertions: verbatim pass-through, membership-enumerate, selective kill, duplicate-refusal, two-pi concurrency proof via capture-pane matching pinned pi-ready marker 'escape interrupt' in BOTH panes. rc schema/usage + --output json allowlist updated (ADR-003 D5). Agent Directory Check now classifies every *.md agent file: readable (PASS), host-only broken symlink with target outside cage roots (SKIP+rationale, like host-only skill symlinks), or genuinely corrupt — broken symlink targeting inside a cage root or unreadable non-symlink (still FAIL). Iterates all agents, not just the first. Harness: new host-side fixture suite tests/test-agent-readability.sh (17 assertions) proves all three branches + the suite-level 0-failures contract for an all-dotpi-symlink cage; wired into run-host.sh, BSD/GNU-portable (pure-bash normalization, no realpath -m). Fresh-context adversarial-review: core classification + portability + dual-path sourcing PASS; REVISE on a phantom-PASS output-integrity defect and a test-honesty gap — both fixed (reporting decision factored into shared helper + asserted at the reporting layer; inversion sanity-check confirms the new tests bite). verdict:pass. Shipped via isolated worktree to avoid colliding with rip-cage-swv (disjoint files), ff-merged to main commit 48bc3c2.

Shipped: concurrent two-pi agent_mail round-trip proven live. Two distinct concurrently-live pi agents via rc agent (openrouter provider); agent A sends via 'am mail send' over pi's bash tool while B iteratively polls 'am mail inbox' (≥2 pi-driven invocations) and reads A's exact body — body-EQUALITY on a unique per-run sentinel. pi-native (am CLI over bash, no MCP, no Claude). Harness: tests/test-agent-mail-concurrent.sh (NEEDS_CONTAINER + RC_E2E), own isolated fixture, exits non-zero on failure. Fresh-context adversarial review PASS after two REJECT rounds folded (pi-no-MCP premise; pi-as-launcher false-green). Follow-ups filed: rip-cage-gucm (doc-fix), rip-cage-9yg0 (PI_VERSION), rip-cage-0wbk (ADR promote).

ADR authored. ADR-019 D9 (FLEXIBLE) added: bash-only agents reach in-cage daemons via the daemon's own CLI over bash, not MCP — full predicates (rationale, alternatives w/ warrant tags, what-would-invalidate, canonical_refs). ADR-005 D7 IN-CAGE-DAEMON wording reconciled in place: mcp_fragment serves MCP-capable agents (Claude) only; bash-only agents (pi) use the daemon CLI. INDEX.md row + cross-refs updated. Firmness path: FLEXIBLE (announce+proceed, human-greenlit). Files: docs/decisions/ADR-019-pi-coding-agent-support.md, ADR-005-ecosystem-tools.md, INDEX.md.

Doc-fix shipped: docs/reference/agent-mail-daemon.md now documents the am CLI message surface (am mail send/inbox/read + am agents register, flags verified against tests/test-agent-mail-concurrent.sh), the two reach paths (MCP-capable agents via mcp_fragment vs bash-only agents via the am CLI), and the daemon-mode -32002 gotcha (mcp-agent-mail serve forbids CLI mutating calls; am serve-http --no-auth is the CLI-friendly mode). Cross-refs ADR-019 D9 + ADR-005 D7. Existing content preserved.

Decision: PI_VERSION stays latest (user wants fresh pi; pinning rejected). Bounded the safety risk with a content-based guard-parity re-verify check in tests/test-pi-dcg-gate.sh section 5 (5a positive sentinel: bash.js dist has bashSchema+command field; 5b: no alternative exec-field name in bash schema; 5c: allToolNames parsed-set sentinel + unexpected-tool drift). FAIL altitude, fires only on real exec-surface drift (not harmless version bumps), reachable via rc test. Sentinel-gated + hardened against vacuous pass (5c validates against the PARSED Set, fails-closed if the dist format changes / parse yields no bash). Fire-tested: fails on missing-file/renamed-schema/renamed-field/alt-field/new-tool/multi-line-Set; passes on real pi 0.73.1 (confirms guard premise still holds past the old 0.70.2). ADR-019 D8/D9 reconciled: states pi tracks latest with this tripwire, no longer implies a pinned version. shellcheck clean.

Stale in_progress marker. Investigation + design deliverable shipped: docs/2026-04-20-egress-firewall-design.md + ADR-012-egress-firewall.md (L7 TLS-MITM denylist-first proxy). The egress firewall itself has since been implemented and is default-on (ADR-012 D1/D8/D10, init-firewall.sh, rip_cage_egress.py). Acceptance (design doc + implement-vs-defer decision) fully met long ago; this marker was never flipped.

Fixed test-integration.sh step 10: replaced stale 'test -L' (legacy symlink-only) assertions with a symlink-OR-bind-mountpoint check matching init-rip-cage.sh _is_mountpoint (/proc/mounts), so it passes against both dn2 (bind-mount) and legacy (.claude-state volume) cages. Verified against live dn2 cage (personal-rip-cage): projects+sessions probe exits 0 (mountpoint). False-green guard: negative control on a plain dir exits 1, so the check is not weakened to a trivial test -d. bash -n clean; file not in shellcheck gate. Adversarial-review: low-stakes mechanical test fix, verified with positive+negative empirical controls against a real cage — noticing recorded here in lieu of fresh-context dispatch.

Resolved via ADR-024 D6 (commit 21545e1): CLI-over-bash is rip-cage's blessed in-cage integration path; third-party MCP = acknowledged residual risk, no trust machinery built. Landed as the research bead's option (b).

Deleted stale test-ssh-translator.sh (asserted dropped pre-ADR-022 known-hosts rewrite); wired authoritative test-ssh-config.sh into run-host.sh — CHECK 5 (ADR-022 D4 inverse: UserKnownHostsFile/GlobalKnownHostsFile NOT rewritten) now runs GREEN in the host suite (empirical control, verified by implementer running bash tests/run-host.sh). Sibling dangling comment refs fixed. Mechanical edit + live empirical control = no separate adversarial-review dispatch; scoping review covered the set. Committed 6de5d72. Broader unwired-host-test audit → rip-cage-b6ia. Note: host suite has 4 pre-existing failures unrelated to this bead (test-rc-commands.sh, test-completions.sh, test-auto-seed.sh, test-manifest-agent-mail.sh).

Added tests/test-allowed-roots-bypass.sh (5 cases) pinning current allowed-roots behavior; all green against current rc, NO rc changes. Cases: (1)(2) symlink workspace/env-file outside roots → rejected after realpath w/ 'outside allowed roots' stderr; (3) .beads/redirect outside roots → graceful ignore (warning+continue+no bad mount, pins rc:1751), NOT hard reject — acceptance corrected from the inaccurate 'rejected with stderr' wording per scoping review; (4) non-existent leaf under symlinked outside-roots parent → rejected (orchestrator tightened to assert the rejection MESSAGE, not just nonzero exit, closing a residual false-green gap); (5) positive control (inside-roots accepted) guards against vacuous all-reject pass. Exercises REAL rc functions; negative control demonstrated (flipping case-1 target in-roots produced a real FAIL+exit1). Wired into run-host.sh. Committed 395ca1c. False-green-proof per the repo's silent-red discipline (positive+negative controls present).

Fixed inverted FAIL polarity in test-pi-dcg-gate.sh check 4b (mcp_exec sensitivity proof). The check FAILed when dcg conservatively DENIED the unknown tool_name mcp_exec — but deny is the SAFER outcome (dcg catching the unknown tool directly), so a legitimate future dcg hardening would have read as a false regression. Now both dcg regimes PASS with regime-specific notes (fail-open→pinning load-bearing; deny→belt-and-suspenders). Diagnostic-signal-quality only; guard correctness unaffected (4a remains the real pinning assertion). Committed bf14765.

HARNESS — named skip (ADR-006 D4 / ADR-012 D3 trivial-shape exemption, with stronger rationale than 'trivial'): the changed branch is UNREACHABLE by the real-cage harness. The current dcg fails-open on mcp_exec → the test takes the ALLOW branch (unchanged by this edit, still PASS); the edited DENY branch only fires if dcg DENIES mcp_exec, which no current pi+dcg version does. So ./rc build + rc up + rc test would exercise only the unchanged allow path and produce identical output — zero discriminating power for this change. Verification done: bash -n clean + polarity flip correct by inspection (if-deny→pass) + image-exercised allow branch untouched and green. A rebuild adds no signal over static review here.

agent+sessions backfilled into completions/rc.bash and completions/_rc (correct per-command format, no reorders). test-completions.sh 12 PASS / 0 FAIL. Verified by parent re-verify + final combined host-suite run.

Test 19 rewritten to swap a REAL inspectable alpine image (stale version label) over rip-cage:latest — containerd-compatible, unlike the old FROM-scratch stub that silently no-op'd. Positive sentinel proves the staleness path actually fired (installed=stale-test-0.0.0 != rc=0.6.0); Test 20 ELSE branch confirmed reached. Both mutations (live image + VERSION-file rename — env RC_VERSION override doesn't work, rc always re-reads the file) now guarded by trap EXIT/INT/TERM so an interrupt can't leave the user's rip-cage:latest stale or VERSION renamed. Post-run: rip-cage:latest restored to 0.6.0, VERSION clean.

S2 sandbox now pre-seeds BOTH config.yaml and tools.yaml; asserts no seeding notice (grep) AND no rewrite (mtime) for BOTH seed sources — root cause was an under-seeded fixture (only config.yaml) tripping a legitimate first-time tool-manifest seed. tools.yaml mtime teeth confirmed via forced-fail simulation. S1-S5 green.

T1b/c/d (and the e2e-gated T2e straggler) switched from bare python3 to 'uv run [--with pyyaml] python' — host system python3 lacked PyYAML under the repo's uv-only convention, and stderr suppression hid the ModuleNotFoundError as an empty extraction. Suppression removed so a future interpreter-env break surfaces loud. FAILURES=0; fixture untouched.

Host-tier suite back to full green (run-host.sh --host-only, exit 0, no reds beyond the original 4 — confirmed complete set). ZERO production regressions: 1 product-artifact drift (completions missing agent/sessions, fix-code) + 3 test/env artifacts (containerd image-store fixture incompat, under-seeded auto-seed fixture, bare-python3 PyYAML absence under uv-only). Pre-dispatch scoping review pre-empted 2 false-green guards (Test 19 vacuous-skip, S2 grep-narrowing); parent re-verify caught 3 hardening findings (crash-safe trap for live-image+VERSION mutation, tools.yaml mtime coverage, T2e uv straggler), all folded + re-verified. Live rip-cage:latest + VERSION intact post-run.

cm-builder Dockerfile stage cross-compiles cm v0.2.11 for linux-arm64 (Bun 1.3.14 --compile --target=bun-linux-arm64, CM_REF 2e63e9b); runtime COPYs only the binary. Proven in built arm64 image: cm --version=0.2.11 rc=0, cm context --json valid JSON rc=0, /usr/local/bin/cm root-owned, no toolchain leak. rc build green. Reviewer PASS (2 non-blocking nice-to-haves; --depth=1 deliberately NOT applied — unsafe for arbitrary pinned SHA). Committed b63951d.

Managed RW mount of host cm store → /home/agent/.cass-memory via _up_prepare_docker_mounts (plain -v, not SFL scan root; host path resolved by cm precedence CASS_MEMORY_HOME→XDG_DATA_HOME/cass-memory→~/.cass-memory; ADR-023 denylist check; fail-loud log). Agent-writable confirmed (Docker Desktop uid-remap, no chown needed); round-trip proven (in-cage cm playbook list shows 25 host entries; cage write visible on host). Mount-only-if-exists (seed removed per review); absent-store warns+skips, no crash, no dir created. ADR-005 D5 discovery: cage-claude.md cm entry + init command-v detection. docs/reference/cm.md documents ADR-024 RW-mount risk. bash -n + shellcheck clean. Reviewer REVISE→all 3 findings fixed. Commits 4cbafbe + 1fbeec1.

tests/test-manifest-cm.sh: gated e2e proof, green against built arm64 cage (RC_E2E=1). Asserts T2a cm --version=0.2.11 rc=0; T2b context --json parseable; T2c playbook-add round-trip (single PASS only after list-reflect + host-store write); T2d /usr/local/bin/cm root-owned + agent-write DENIED; T2e ZERO external TCP connections during cm ops (real committed egress assertion). Round-trips against a THROWAWAY temp store (CASS_MEMORY_HOME), real ~/.local/share/cass-memory untouched; trap cleanup. Wired into tests/run-host.sh; self-skips (named) without RC_E2E/image; .1-regression → FAIL not skip. bash -n + shellcheck clean. Reviewer REVISE→3 findings fixed (F1 missing egress assertion added, F2 PASS-contingent-on-round-trip, F3 sentinel collision). Commits prior + 1a81ea7.

Parent-re-verify drift fixed. F3: cm host path realpath-resolved before denylist + -v arg (ADR-023 D7 FIRM), matching sibling callsites (rc:1611). F4: extracted _cm_build_mount_arg helper (called by _up_prepare_docker_mounts); T3h/T3i exercise the real wiring — symlink→denylist DENIED (rc=2), symlink→safe echoes canonical realpath; proven non-vacuous via revert→RED / restore→GREEN. F6: cm.md repo URL → Dicklesworthstone/cass_memory_system (grep-confirmed). F7: test comment no longer presents host-specific ~/.local/share path as canonical default. F8: ADR-024 citation D1→D5 (D5 carries the non-adversarial acceptance rationale; D1 only admits the threat) in cm.md + rc:1596/1601. RC_E2E=1 test green (11 checks); bash -n + shellcheck clean. Commits c65e2de + a89acb7. Reviewer: F3/F8 PASS round 1, F4 round 2 after seam confirmed closed.

cm (CASSMS) runs end-to-end inside an arm64 cage. Reframed from a design-brainstorm bead to a concrete instantiation once /recall surfaced ADR-005 D7's host-only-manifest mechanism (already shipped, closed epic rip-cage-4c5) — acceptance (1) met by existing FIRM substrate. Children: .1 Bun cross-compile builder stage (cm v0.2.11, no linux-arm64 release → bun --compile --target, no emulation); .2 host cm store mounted RW via _cm_build_mount_arg helper (path resolved by cm precedence; ADR-023 denylist; agent-writable proven, round-trip to host store); .4 gated e2e (cm --version + context --json + playbook round-trip in throwaway store + binary-ownership + zero-egress); .5 fixed parent-re-verify drift. Parent re-verify (2 rounds) caught conjunction-drift per-child reviews missed: ADR-023 D7 realpath-before-denylist (FIRM), bypass-path false-green test, ADR-024 D1→D5 citation across 3 files, cm-as-bundled-default → ADR-005 D2/D6 evolved in place (commit 3773185). Follow-ups filed: rip-cage-ywek (arch-adaptive Bun target — amd64 hosts), rip-cage-hqvk (ADR-005 D3 versions.env vs reality, bumped P1). /compound done: 3 memory folds (silent-red DP8 bypass-path false-green, adr-citation-grep-sweep, /recall-collapses-brainstorm calibration) + harness.md convention. Commits b63951d, 4cbafbe, 1fbeec1, 4546df6, 1a81ea7, c65e2de, a89acb7, 3773185, 1b3ac78, dbe14c8.

Audited all 20 dark test-*.sh; wired 8 host-tier + 5 container-tier into run-host.sh, fixed 4 (EXIT-trap + 3 RC_CONFIG_GLOBAL shadow), deleted 2 superseded (ported mise coverage forward), documented 5 as run-elsewhere. Full host suite green (651 PASS, exit 0). Adversarial review folded (3 orphan-ref fixes). Compounded. Follow-up rip-cage-nu91 filed. Commits 99e8438 + 6f32bda.

Manifest mounts consumer shipped. _manifest_build_mount_args emits -v host:dest (two-element form, cm parity); mounts[] schema is now {host,dest} with _manifest_validate rejecting malformed elements and _manifest_check_mounts_denylist reading .host (ADR-023 fail-closed, effect-tested). Host-side green (test-manifest-mounts.sh) + RC_E2E ME1 GREEN: manifest-declared mount present inside a real running cage. Two critical review defects fixed (broken -v array arg; dead denylist guard) + MC2 made effect-based.

Generic from-source builder stage shipped. Manifest build_source entry (builder_image + host-side build_script + output_path) generates ONE isolated builder stage injected before the runtime FROM, COPY'ing only the artifact (toolchain absent from runtime); arch-adaptive (no --platform/--target, subsumes ywek). _manifest_validate extended to accept from-source fields and permit no install_cmd. Host-side green (test-manifest-source.sh) + RC_E2E SE1 GREEN: real build produced a running native-arch binary with toolchain absent. Two ADR-024 review gaps fixed (bundled+build_source silently accepted; newline-injection guard missing on the three new fields).

Validator extended with binary-root-owned (post-build, effect-based docker-run-stat) + build-isolation (pre-build Dockerfile scan: bind/VOLUME/ssh/secret mounts) assertions, fail-closed (ADR-001/ADR-005 D11 FIRM). Host-side green (test-manifest-security.sh) + RC_E2E BE1/BE2 GREEN: compliant build passes (root 755), hostile chmod-777 build REJECTED with D9 error, falsifiability proven RED->GREEN, F1 tainted-image-untag verified. Review-caught: tainted image left tagged (fixed); ssh/secret isolation vectors (added). install_cmd root-owned under-coverage filed as follow-up rip-cage-ryn6.
subsumed by rip-cage-buuo.2 generic arch-adaptive from-source builder stage; cm re-expressed as a from-source manifest entry with no hardcoded --target (rip-cage-buuo.5)

cm demoted to manifest worked example. Removed cm-builder Dockerfile stage + runtime COPY, the hardcoded rc wiring (_resolve_cass_memory_host_path, _cm_build_mount_arg, cm mount block), and the init-rip-cage.sh cm detection block. cm re-expressed as a from-source manifest example (arch-adaptive build script, no --target; store mount). RC_E2E verified: default build has NO cm (D8); cm-via-manifest runs (0.2.11), round-trips to host store THROUGH the real manifest consumer, binary root-owned, zero external TCP. Worked-example integration gap surfaced + fixed: added ~//Users/jonatanpi expansion to the .1 mounts consumer (one helper, both consumer + denylist pre-check) so the example is copy-paste functional; denylist runs post-expansion (MD2 security test proves a ~-expanded denylisted path is still rejected). Docs (cm.md, cage-claude.md) reflect opt-in-via-manifest. ywek subsumed/closed.

Repo-shipped authoring skill landed at .agents/skills/rip-cage-tool-manifest-author/SKILL.md. A host-side agent, pointed at a target tool, drafts a manifest entry (+ build script for from-source) as human-reviewable files under ~/.config/rip-cage; human approves before rc build; fail-closed validator enforces. D7 host-only / no-in-cage-write / no-runtime-injection stated explicitly (ADR-005 D7, ADR-024). cm is the baked worked example (no prebuilt linux-arm64 -> from-source). Verified: the skill's cm worked example passes _manifest_validate (SA5 + cross-check T3c), no invented schema fields (SA7 cross-checks every field against the rc validator), host suite green. EXPLORATORY firmness (D11 mechanism 3); deeper skill-accuracy read folded into the epic parent re-verify.

Parent-re-verify conjunction-drift fixes shipped. F1 (FIRM): the rc-up auto-build path _pull_or_build now routes both docker-build branches through _pull_or_build_local, wiring _manifest_check_build_isolation (pre) + _manifest_check_binary_root_owned (post, untag-on-fail) — same fail-closed semantics as cmd_build, so from-source tools built via rc-up-without-prior-rc-build get full D11 enforcement (ADR-005 D11 mechanism 2). RC_E2E BE3 proves a crafted-bad from-source tool via the auto-build path is REJECTED with no tainted image; compliant passes. grep confirms only cmd_build + _pull_or_build_local call docker build, both guarded (no third bypass). F2: _manifest_validate now rejects a build_script resolving outside the build context (absolute, ../-escape, bare ..) fail-loud (ADR-001) — S13/S14/S15; SKILL.md corrected (no absolute-path claim). F3: vestigial /home/agent/.cass-memory removed from the default-image mkdir. Reviewed PASS (refactor behavior-preserving, pull/cmd_build paths unchanged).

Agent-first tool composability (ADR-005 D11) shipped. All 6 children closed: mounts consumer (.1, +~/$HOME expansion), generic from-source builder stage (.2, subsumes ywek), fail-closed validator binary-root-owned + build-isolation (.3), repo-shipped authoring skill (.4), cm demoted to manifest worked example (.5), and the parent-re-verify FIRM-bypass fix (.6, rc-up auto-build path now validated). Verified host-side AND on real RC_E2E=1 builds (mount parity in-cage, from-source native-arch binary toolchain-absent, crafted-bad rejected with D9 error via both build entrypoints). Pre-impl scoping review + per-child fresh-context review + epic parent re-verify (caught the FIRM bypass, fixed in .6). /compound folded 2 L2B memories; Promote landed as ADR-005 D11 entrypoint-completeness clause (FIRM, human-confirmed). Follow-up rip-cage-ryn6 filed (install_cmd root-owned coverage). 4 impl commits + 1 ADR commit.

Close-and-spawn at brainstorm convergence (2026-06-13). All three acceptance items met: (1) operating model D1-D5 recorded in dotclaude bd memory mac-mini-claude-session-persistence-mosh-tmux (re-created with current framing, PROVISIONAL/mini-down stripped — mini synced, clone reconciled). (2) Cross-project verification DONE: rip-cage ADRs + code re-scouted post-sync; D1-D5 operating model (mosh wire, herdr-in-cage supervisor, layered cmux→mosh→rc→multiplexer composition) re-CONFIRMED, untouched by the 58 merged commits. (3) D6 re-derived + spawned: the old 'make the in-cage multiplexer pluggable / swap in herdr' D6 rested on a DEAD premise ('cage has no multi-agent lever') — rc agent + per-session config isolation shipped in v0.7.0. Re-derived against rc-agent reality + a from-source herdr investigation (herdr is its own multiplexer, not a tmux layer) into rip-cage-1f59 (epic, verdict:pass): 'get rc out of the multiplexer business — in-cage multiplexer becomes a swappable composed tool, default none, retire rc agent/sessions.' The operating model's herdr-in-cage want is satisfied by 1f59's 'pick herdr' path.

Moot: the VS Code devcontainer path (rc init) is being removed (decided 2026-06-13, tracked in the new VS-Code-removal bead). The maintainer never uses the VS Code path and won't support it, so 'forward ssh-agent via the rc init devcontainer path' has no consumer. ssh-agent forwarding remains on the rc up / CLI path per ADR-017 D1 (unaffected). If VS Code support is ever re-added deliberately, re-file parity then.

Closed as stale (2026-06-13). This 2026-05-29 session handoff/tracking map for the symlink-config / secret-path-denylist thread has gone stale: it lists the concurrent-agents-clobber-shared-config failure (rip-cage-p1p) as open/related, but p1p SHIPPED + closed 2026-06-08 (per-session Claude config isolation). Its embedded snapshot of the active security frontier predates ADR-026 (containment + delegated mediation). The substantive symlink-config/secret-path items it tracked live in their own open beads (a0h mount-hygiene, 6uz scope=parent symlink-follow); the handoff-map function is served by those + bd show. No unabsorbed work lost.

Config seam + init multiplexer-server lifecycle shipped. session.multiplexer (none|tmux|herdr, default none) parsed via the selection_list schema path (fail-loud on invalid enum, ADR-001); threaded into the container as RC_MULTIPLEXER env + rc.session.multiplexer label in cmd_up; init-rip-cage.sh tmux-start block replaced by a multiplexer-dispatch case (tmux=server+rip-cage session, herdr=stub for .5, none=nothing). Verified COLD (rc build -> rc up <fresh>): under none ps shows no tmux/herdr server, under tmux the server + rip-cage session exist; invalid enum exits 1 with the allowed-values error. Host-tier T40/T41/T42 + bash -n + shellcheck(v0.11.0) clean. Fresh-context review: clean, no blocking issues (migration-noise wrinkle filed as .9). kt25's VS-Code guard preserved.

Box-entry made multiplexer-aware + rc exec shipped. check_tmux gated (fires only for tmux; up reads workspace config, attach/exec read the rc.session.multiplexer label, sessions/agent still require tmux until .3 removes them). Attach helpers + 3 cmd_up blocks + cmd_attach dispatch on multiplexer (plain shell for none, tmux attach for tmux, herdr-client stub for .5). New cmd_exec runs via docker exec, propagates exit, ships --output json, works non-TTY. Fresh-context review found a BLOCKING ADR-003 D1 violation (cmd_exec command-stdout contaminated the json envelope) + 3 quality issues — all repaired; re-verified LIVE: rc --output json exec | jq parses clean (command output to stderr), non-json prints to stdout, false->exit 1. T48-T52 + test-prerequisites T2 (tmux-gated) + make test + bash -n + make lint clean. agent/sessions left intact for .3.

rc agent + rc sessions retired from every surface: cmd_agent/cmd_sessions defs deleted; removed from dispatch case, --output json allowlist, check_tmux gate, check_docker gate, usage heredoc, rc schema, and both completion files. No dead case arm (grep empty). Both commands -> unknown-command exit 1; schema keys: attach/auth/build/completions/config/destroy/doctor/down/exec/init/ls/reload/schema/setup/test/up (no agent/sessions, exec + mgmt intact). Guard-removal audit: no internal callers. Fresh-context review confirmed behavior clean but flagged VACUOUS tests (asserted docker-error exit, not retirement) + missing agent coverage — repaired to non-vacuous absence assertions (schema has() / help / completions) symmetric for both commands; re-verified in own shell (make test + test-completions green). NOTE: test-rc-commands Test 42 (cli-reference.md documents rc sessions) is stale-true debt flagged onto .6 to invert when docs are updated.

herdr installable via the tool manifest + tmux dropped as a hard Homebrew dep. Added a herdr TOOL manifest entry to the seed (_manifest_default_yaml) — version-pinned v0.6.10 prebuilt download with SHA-256 verification (fail-closed), egress github.com, root-owned binary. Dropped depends_on tmux from Formula (tmux stays baked in image, runs only when selected — symmetric with herdr). init-rip-cage.sh herdr branch wired to start 'herdr server' (was a stub), gated on RC_MULTIPLEXER=herdr. Fresh-context review caught a supply-chain gap (no checksum; fixture falsely claimed none published) -> repaired with cross-verified per-arch sha256 (GitHub API digest + local sha256sum). GATING signal evidenced on the FINAL checksummed state via a real RC_E2E=1 build: build succeeds (proves checksums correct), herdr 0.6.10 root-owned at /usr/local/bin/herdr, server PID running under herdr-multiplexer, socket present. T1 + make lint + bash -n clean. Resolved the parent D7 validation item: herdr exposes HERDR_SESSION identity env (finding passed to .4 -> herdr isolation branch is gating).

Per-session Claude config isolation re-keyed multiplexer-agnostic. claude-session-wrapper.sh + zshrc derive CLAUDE_CONFIG_DIR via 4-way precedence (explicit CLAUDE_CONFIG_DIR override -> tmux session identity -> HERDR_SESSION -> default fallback), in lockstep. p1p config-isolation probe (test-claude-concurrency.sh) extended with Step 7: 7a none->default, 7b tmux->per-session, 7c GATING herdr->per-HERDR_SESSION (inode-isolated, uses fail() not skip). herdr branch is gating (not fallback) since .5 confirmed HERDR_SESSION. 25 assertions green. Fresh-context review found the 7c inode-isolation check could silently skip if default/.claude.json absent -> fixed with an else-fail so the gating check never silently passes; bash -n clean. Verified via warm-cage docker-cp (valid for a wrapper-exec change, not init-ordering) + env-injection unit test of the derivation.

Docs + agent-context updated for the new multiplexer surface. Removed all live rc agent/rc sessions references from CLAUDE.md, README.md, docs/reference/{cli-reference,whats-in-the-box,config}.md. Documented rc exec (+ --output json), session.multiplexer config row (mirrors ADR-021 D6), multiplexer-neutral rc attach (none=plain shell/tmux=session/herdr=supervisor). CLAUDE.md Two-usage-paths + Architecture reconciled; devcontainer-path description left intact for kt25 (not authored twice). Inverted test-rc-commands Test 42 (cli-reference now asserts ABSENCE of rc sessions + rc agent) — non-vacuous. Fresh-context review found the rc exec --output json synopsis showed the flag in the wrong (post --) position -> corrected to 'rc --output json exec <cage> -- <cmd>'. rg confirms no live stale refs; new surface documented; make test green.

e2e multiplexer-lifecycle harness shipped (the parent's integration Signal). test-multi-agent-levers.sh (which asserted the removed rc agent/sessions) DELETED — restructured into tests/test-multiplexer-lifecycle.sh, parameterized over session.multiplexer. Assertions (a) none: plain shell + enumerated procs (no tmux/herdr server) + two independent rc exec terminals + close-one-leaves-other; (b) tmux: detach/reattach survival; (c) retirement absent from dispatch/help/schema/completions/json-allowlist; (d) config-isolation p1p probe green under none/tmux + GATING herdr. herdr server spawn asserted; status-view a visible SKIP. run-host.sh rewired (NEEDS_CONTAINER :42 + anchored path :179; old filename grep-empty). FAIL->exit 1; cleanup trap tears down all 3 cages. Fresh-context review caught a BLOCKING macOS cleanup leak (label realpath mismatch -> leaked cages) + 3 minors (vacuous-pass guard, unanchored completions grep, lint-coverage gap+SC2001) -> all fixed; re-verified via a real RC_E2E run: (a)-(d) green AND post-run rc ls shows zero leaked mux-test cages. make lint now covers the new file.

herdr attach client wired (discovered-from parent re-verify; closes parent clause 2 'rc attach lands in the herdr client'). Replaced the 4 'exit 1 not yet implemented (pending 1f59.5)' stubs (3 cmd_up attach branches + cmd_attach) with TTY-guarded dispatch into the running herdr client: TTY -> 'docker exec -it <cage> herdr' (opens the supervisor TUI against the init-started server socket), non-TTY -> stderr attach-hint + exit 0 (mirrors none/tmux branches), --new/--session warned as ignored. Stub-grep empty; cli-reference.md:101 'supervisor view' now accurate. Fresh-context review clean (dispatch consistent across 4 sites, no error-masking, none/tmux/exec branches untouched). bash -n + make lint clean; RC_E2E multiplexer-lifecycle green; non-TTY rc attach confirmed (hint not stub-error).

Repo-wide drift sweep complete (discovered-from parent re-verify; closes parent clause 6 ADR-canonicalization + removes dangling consumers). CHANGELOG [Unreleased] Removed entry (rc agent/sessions -> rc exec + in-cage multiplexer); ADR-006 canonical_refs line no longer cites rc sessions as D7 lever (now rc exec + ADR-021 D6); ADR-003 D5 example adds exec + 'illustrative, rc schema authoritative' disclaimer; .claude/harness.md subcommands add exec+reload+config (matches live 16-cmd schema); docs/2026-03-27 design doc gets a dated deprecation note (no history rewrite). Repo-wide grep sweep found + fixed a 7th surface the re-verify reviewer missed: tests/test-agent-mail-concurrent.sh had live 'rc agent ... -- pi' spawns -> replaced with the equivalent 'docker exec tmux new-session -d -s X pi' (what rc agent wrapped). Sweep now clean (all remaining hits are historical/retirement-framed). Fresh-context review clean; make test + make lint green. KNOWN ITEM: the test-agent-mail-concurrent.sh substitution is mechanically equivalent + bash -n clean + session-names consistent, but that test is RC_E2E+auth-gated and was NOT exercised at close — flag for the next agent_mail e2e run.

SHIPPED. rip-cage decoupled from tmux: the in-cage multiplexer is now a swappable composed tool (session.multiplexer: none|tmux|herdr, default none). All 6 acceptance clauses met + re-verified end-to-end (bash -n + make lint + make test + RC_E2E multiplexer-lifecycle green). 9 children: .1 config seam + init mux-server lifecycle; .2 box-entry (rc exec + multiplexer-neutral rc attach + check_tmux gating); .3 retire rc agent/rc sessions from every surface; .4 multiplexer-agnostic config-isolation re-key (HERDR_SESSION gating); .5 herdr via tool manifest (sha256-verified, root-owned) + tmux dropped as hard Homebrew dep; .6 docs; .8 e2e multiplexer-lifecycle harness (replaced test-multi-agent-levers.sh); + discovered-from .10 (herdr attach client — the gap the parent re-verify caught) and .11 (repo-wide stale-ref + command-inventory drift sweep).
